name: Try-On
description: Virtual try-on for fashion / glasses / makeup / shoes / jewelry / hairstyles. The model stands in front of a mirror or directly faces the camera, then 'tries on' multiple variants in rapid sequence — walk-into-camera + outfit change, snap-fingers swap, mirror-flash cut, or makeup-application timelapse → reveal. 8-25s, 9:16 vertical, 3-8 looks at 2-3s each. The conversion-driving format for fashion / beauty / eyewear / accessory ecommerce — viewer projects 'how would this look on me' without commitment. Reference-required: real garment / real product photos must be supplied, otherwise generation refuses (no AI-improvised branded apparel, ever).

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — character keyframe with consistent face / body / lighting / camera angle across ALL variants. Use ONE seed model image as ref, then generate N variant frames swapping ONLY the product (garment / glasses / lip color / earring). Drift in face or pose between variants is the #1 failure mode of this format.
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter, 2-3s clips per variant (3-8 total). Motion vocabulary: slow turn, mirror flash, outfit-change spin, walk-into-camera. generate_audio: false.
stackSummary.voiceover: Usually NONE. Text-overlay product names + price carry the spot. If VO is requested: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2, single short tag-line at the final 'winner' beat.
stackSummary.captions: No spoken-line captions. Remotion text-overlay only — large bold sans-serif product name + SKU + price, anchored bottom-third, fades on each variant beat.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — single trend-bed (upbeat pop / hip-hop / electronic) with a clean BPM. Transitions land on the beat; the format dies if cuts are off-beat. Optional: licensed trend audio for TikTok-native uploads.

constants.structure: 0-2s base + first variant → 2-20s variant cycle on beat → 20-25s winner hold
constants.overlayStyle: bottom-third large bold sans-serif, product + price

variationAxes: try-on category (fashion / glasses / makeup / jewelry / hair / shoes)
with-price-overlay (yes — ecommerce conversion / no — pure vibe)
framing (mirror selfie / front-cam direct / full-body wide)
model archetype (real ref photo / persona archetype / generic AI model)
doNotCopyLiterally: Scenario, on-screen text, exact prompts, model identity. Vibe reference only.
referenceNotes: Real garment / product photos REQUIRED. The reference-required gate (AGENTS.md hard rule #3) refuses if no reference exists at workspace/projects/<id>/assets/uploaded/. AI-improvised branded apparel, hallucinated frame shapes, or made-up lipstick shades always read as fake and never match the actual SKU the user is selling. Generic / fictional collections may proceed without a reference — log as stage: 'no-ref-consent' and warn the user.
